person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Elizabeth Gordon, Heiress of Gordon († 1439), Scottish baroness and progenitress of the Gordon Earls and Marquesses of Huntly.

«b»Life«/b»
Elizabeth Gordon was the daughter of Adam de Gordon, Lord of Gordon[a] and Elizabeth Keith, daughter of William Keith, Marischal of Scotland.

Elizabeth, underage at the time of her father's death, was a ward of Walter Haliburton of Dirleton. Sir William Seton purchased her wardship on 7 March 1408 for a liferent of 50 merks from the barony of Tranent. Sir William betrothed her to his eldest son Sir John Seton but he declined preferring a daughter of the Earl of March; Elizabeth was then married to his younger brother, Alexander Seton, who in 1406 was a prisoner along with the future king James I of Scotland.

Elizabeth and Alexander wed in 1408. He acquired through his marriage to Elizabeth Gordon the lands of Gordon and Huntly, confirmed to them on 20 July 1408. This was according to custom of the time that Elizabeth Gordon, of her own free will, resigned her lands before Parliament at Perth and she and the Regent Albany issued a new charter of "All and whole of the lands and baronies of Gordon and Huntly lying within the sheriffdom of Berwick; the lands of Fogo and Faunes with their pertinents; the lands of Strathbogie and Beldygordon with the pertinents in Aberdeenshire; to be held by the said Alexander and Elizabeth and their heirs lawfully procreated; whom failing the true and lawful heirs of the said Elizabeth whomsoever; rendering the services used and wont."

Three years later, Alexander fought at the Battle of Harlaw and was knighted before 1419. In 1421/2 he traveled to France and visited King James of Scotland. Alexander Seton was one of those who negotiated for the release of the Scottish monarch and was a hostage for his king, but was released after a year in England to return to his family in Scotland. In that same year Alexander and Elizabeth were granted a charter for half the lands of Culclarochy and part of Gerry in the barony of Drumblade. About 1436 Alexander was created a Lord of Parliament as Alexander Seton, Lord Gordon.

In 1428, Alexander and Elizabeth were granted a dispensation from the pope long after their marriage when it was determined they were within the forbidden degrees of consanguinity; the dispensation stating that Alexander Seton had contracted marriage with Elizabeth, the heiress of Gordon, "per verba de presenti publici, juxta morem patriae" (Latin: publicly in accordance with all customs of the time). Elizabeth died at Strathbogie on 16 March 1439 while Alexander died in 1440-41. Elizabeth was buried at St. Nicholas Church in Aberdeen.

«b»Family
The children of Alexander Seton and Elizabeth Gordon were:«/b»
1.) Alexander Seton († 1470), succeeded his parents, took the name of Gordon and was created 1st Earl of Huntly

2.) William Seton († 1452), married Elizabeth, daughter and heiress of William Meldrum of Meldrum, ancestor of the Setons of Meldrum

3.) Henry Seton († 1452) killed with his brother William at the Battle of Brechin

4.) Elizabeth Seton, married to Alexander of Islay, Earl of Ross († 1449)

sourceList.title: London, England, Baptisms, Marriages and Burials, 1538-1812

zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Gordon Heiress of Gordon於1384年出生在英國蘇格蘭阿爾迪史爾亨特利時，她的父親Sir Adam De Gordon28歲，母親Elisabeth De Keith24歲。她於1401年與Alexander Seton Lord Gordon在英國蘇格蘭阿爾迪史爾亨特利結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和7個女兒的父母。她去世於1438年3月16日在蘇格蘭阿爾迪史爾亨特利享年54歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭阿爾迪史爾阿爾迪。

nameMeanings.name: Gordon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from Gordon in Berwickshire, named with Welsh gor ‘spacious’ + din ‘fort’.
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Gourdon in Saône-et-Loire, so called from the Gallo-Roman personal name Gordus + the locative suffix -o, -ōnis.
English (of Norman origin): alternatively, said to be a nickname from a diminutive of Old French gourd ‘heavy, dull, sluggish’ (compare 8 below).

variants.name: Gordin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Eyal, Moisey, Yakov, Zalman, Erez, Faina, Haya, Mendel.  Russian Leonid, Aleksandr, Boris, Feiga, Genady.
French: variant of   Gourdin  . This surname is very rare in France.

variants.name: Gordan
meanings: German: variant of Jordan . The surname Gordan is very rare in Germany.
Irish (Wexford and Sligo): adopted as an Anglicized form of Mag Mhuirneacháin ‘son of Muirneachán’, a personal name from a diminutive of muirneach ‘lovable’.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Gordon Heiress of Gordon was born in 1384, in Huntly, Aberdeenshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, her father, Sir Adam De Gordon, was 28 and her mother, Elisabeth De Keith, was 24. She married Alexander Seton Lord Gordon in 1401, in Huntly, Aberdeenshire, Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 7 daughters. She died on 16 March 1438, in Huntly, Aberdeenshire, Scotland, at the age of 54, and was buried in Aberdeen, Aberdeenshire, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Gordon Heiress of Gordon est née en 1384, à Huntly, Aberdeenshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Adam De Gordon, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Elisabeth De Keith, avait 24 ans. Elle a épousé Alexander Seton Lord Gordon en 1401, à Huntly, Aberdeenshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 7 filles. Elle est décédée le 16 mars 1438, à Huntly, Aberdeenshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 54 ans, et a été enterrée à Aberdeen, Aberdeenshire, Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Gordon Heiress of Gordon 1384 in Huntly, Aberdeenshire, Schottland, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Adam De Gordon 28 und ihre Mutter Elisabeth De Keith 24. Sie hat 1401 in Huntly, Aberdeenshire, Schottland, United Kingdom Alexander Seton Lord Gordon geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 7 Töchter. Sie starb am 16. März 1438 im Alter von 54 Jahren in Huntly, Aberdeenshire, Scotland und wurde in Aberdeen, Aberdeenshire, Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Gordon Heiress of Gordon è nata il nel 1384 nel Huntly, Aberdeenshire, Scozia, Regno Unito, sua padre, Sir Adam De Gordon, aveva 28 anni e sua madre, Elisabeth De Keith, aveva 24 anni. Lei sposato Alexander Seton Lord Gordon nel 1401, nel Huntly, Aberdeenshire, Scozia, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 7 figlie. Morì il 16 marzo 1438, nel Huntly, Aberdeenshire, Scotland, all'età di 54 anni, e fu sepolta a Aberdeen, Aberdeenshire, Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Gordon Heiress of Gordonが1384年にイギリススコットランドアバーディーンシュアイアハントリーで生まれた時，父Sir Adam De Gordonは28歳，母Elisabeth De Keithは24歳であった。1401年にイギリススコットランドアバーディーンシュアイアハントリーでAlexander Seton Lord Gordonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼女は1438年3月16日にスコットランドアバーディーンシュアイアハントリーで54歳で死去し，スコットランドアバーディーンシュアイアアバーディーンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Gordon Heiress of Gordon(이)가 1384년 영국 스코틀랜드 압르디실르 헌틀리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Adam De Gordon(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elisabeth De Keith(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 1401년 영국 스코틀랜드 압르디실르 헌틀리에서 Alexander Seton Lord Gordon(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1438년 3월 16일 스컷랜드 압르디실르 헌틀리에서 54세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 압르디실르 압르디에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Gordon Heiress of Gordon nasceu em 1384, em Huntly, Aberdeenshire, Escócia, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir Adam De Gordon, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Elisabeth De Keith, tinha 24 anos. Ela casou-se com Alexander Seton Lord Gordon em 1401, em Huntly, Aberdeenshire, Escócia, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 7 filhas. Ela faleceu em 16 de março de 1438, em Huntly, Aberdeenshire, Scotland, com 54 anos, e foi sepultada em Aberdeen, Aberdeenshire, Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Gordon Heiress of Gordon родилась в 1384 г. в местности хантли, абирдиншир, Шотландия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Sir Adam De Gordon было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Elisabeth De Keith, 24. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Alexander Seton Lord Gordon в 1401 г. в местности хантли, абирдиншир, Шотландия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 7 дочерей. Она умерла 16 марта 1438 г. в местности хантли, абирдиншир, Шотландия в возрасте 54 лет и была похоронена в местности абирдин, абирдиншир, Шотландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Gordon Heiress of Gordon nació en 1384, en Huntly, Aberdeenshire, Escocia, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir Adam De Gordon, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Elisabeth De Keith, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Alexander Seton Lord Gordon en 1401, en Huntly, Aberdeenshire, Escocia, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 7 hijas. Murió el 16 de marzo de 1438, en Huntly, Aberdeenshire, Scotland, a la edad de 54 años, y fue sepultada en Aberdeen, Aberdeenshire, Scotland.
